codeReview.summary: Rerun review confirms all prior blocking findings from tenant-and-admin round 1 are resolved in commit 4de5b225: API usage counting now uses a single DB-side atomic increment path (no read-then-write race), onboarding no longer persists hardcoded CORS origins, and tenant enabled_modules is now configurable via request DTO + service update logic and a dedicated superadmin modules endpoint.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker-level documentation expectations are still contradictory across mission/skill/session rules, causing procedural drift in fix runs that modify API contracts.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md:13 forbids modifying `.factory/` mission infrastructure while AGENTS.md:101 and backend-worker SKILL.md Step 5 require updating `.factory/library/frontend-handoff.md`; this fix handoff reports `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false` specifically for skipping Step 5 due session rule forbidding doc updates unless explicitly requested.

summary: Pass: compared against prior failed reviews and synthesis blocking findings, this fix adequately closes all four blocking gaps (usage-metrics race, hardcoded onboarding CORS persistence, missing enabled_modules API contract, and missing enabled_modules persistence path). No remaining blocking issues were found in the reviewed fix implementation.
